explanations.1: This starts a test that checks rules about how code in the Controllers folder should behave.
explanations.2: The expect() method tells the test which part of your codebase to examine — in this case, everything in the App\Http\Controllers directory.
explanations.3: The not->toUse() part means 'this code should NOT use this thing'; so controllers aren't allowed to directly use Laravel's Request class.
explanations.7: This targets the Models folder specifically, preparing to set rules about which other parts of the code are allowed to depend on these models.
explanations.8: toOnlyBeUsedOn() means 'the thing we're testing should only ever be imported/used by this other folder'; models should only be used by Repositories.
explanations.9: toOnlyUse() restricts what the Models folder itself is allowed to depend on; models can only import from Illuminate\Database.
explanations.13: This says Repositories can only be used by Controllers — they're internal helpers, not used everywhere.
explanations.17: This is a list of global PHP functions you want to forbid; dd, dump, and var_dump are usually left in by accident during debugging.
explanations.22: The not->toBeUsed() means these facades shouldn't appear anywhere in the codebase.
explanations.23: ignoring() carves out an exception — the rule above doesn't apply to code in the App\Providers folder.
